commit.message:
ci: move gcov detection into CMake coverage-report target (#316)

* ci: move gcov detection into CMake coverage-report target via bilke/cmake-modules

Replace the fragile shell-side CMakeCache.txt parsing (grep | cut, subject
to SIGPIPE and set-e failures) with a cmake/Coverage.cmake module that
detects gcov/llvm-cov at CMake configure time—where CMAKE_CXX_COMPILER and
CMAKE_CXX_COMPILER_ID are natively available.

cmake/Coverage.cmake (new):
- Uses FetchContent to pull bilke/cmake-modules (pinned SHA 5b988b5) and
  includes CodeCoverage.cmake from that repository.
- Detects gcov (GCC) or llvm-cov (Clang) via find_program with HINTS from
  the compiler's own directory, avoiding version mismatches with system
  /usr/bin/gcov-* binaries.
- Pre-sets GCOVR_PATH to the bare "gcovr" command so CodeCoverage's
  FATAL_ERROR does not fire at configure time; gcovr is installed into a
  venv and placed on PATH before the target is invoked.
- Passes --gcov-executable, source --filter patterns, and --json-summary
  via GCOVR_ADDITIONAL_ARGS (each element is one VERBATIM shell argument;
  the space in "llvm-cov gcov" is intentional and handled by gcovr's
  shlex.split).
- Defines a coverage-report target via setup_target_for_coverage_gcovr_html
  that runs ctest (excluding dd4hep label) and gcovr, producing
  build/coverage-report/ and build/coverage-summary.json.
- Adds -fno-inline -fno-omit-frame-pointer alongside the module's standard
  --coverage -g -fprofile-abs-path flags.

CMakeLists.txt:
- Replace the inline add_compile_options/add_link_options block inside
  if(USE_COVERAGE) with include(cmake/Coverage.cmake).

.github/workflows/ci.yml:
- Drop ~50 lines of fragile gcov-detection shell code.
- Install gcovr into a venv, then invoke
  PATH=".venv-gcovr/bin:$PATH" cmake --build build --target coverage-report
- Update artifact and JSON summary paths from workspace-root coverage/ and
  coverage-summary.json to build/coverage-report/ and build/coverage-summary.json.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(cmake/Coverage): avoid GCOV_PATH-NOTFOUND overwrite in CodeCoverage

When find_program(GCOV_PATH llvm-cov ...) fails it writes GCOV_PATH-NOTFOUND
into the CMake cache.  The subsequent set(GCOV_PATH ... CACHE ...) without
FORCE does not override a cached NOTFOUND entry, so CodeCoverage.cmake's own
find_program(GCOV_PATH NAMES gcov) re-runs, also fails, and FATAL_ERRORs.

Fix:
- Use private _g4occt_* variables for detection so GCOV_PATH is never
  left as NOTFOUND before include(CodeCoverage).
- Always set GCOV_PATH with FORCE so CodeCoverage's check passes.
- For Clang when llvm-cov is not found, fall back to system gcov
  (Clang's --coverage flag generates GCOV-compatible .gcno/.gcda files).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(cmake/Coverage): run gcovr even when ctest fails via cmake -P script

commit.message:
Split NuGet mega-packages to stay under 500MB feed limit (#3584)

* Split NuGet mega-packages to stay under 500MB feed limit

The _NuGets, _NuGetsPreview, _Symbols, and _SymbolsPreview packages
exceeded the Azure DevOps 500MB size limit. Split each into:
  - .Managed (managed-only nupkgs, small)
  - .NativeAssets (native asset nupkgs, large)
  - Parent meta-package (depends on both sub-packages)

The download side (DownloadPackageAsync) already follows dependencies,
so consumers automatically get both sub-packages when requesting the
parent.

New nuspec templates:
  _NuGets.Managed.nuspec, _NuGets.NativeAssets.nuspec
  _NuGetsPreview.Managed.nuspec, _NuGetsPreview.NativeAssets.nuspec
  _Symbols.Managed.nuspec, _Symbols.NativeAssets.nuspec
  _SymbolsPreview.Managed.nuspec, _SymbolsPreview.NativeAssets.nuspec

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Replace Managed/NativeAssets split with dynamic bin-packing

Instead of manually splitting _NuGets into .Managed and .NativeAssets
sub-packages, dynamically bin-pack all nupkgs into ~200 MB numbered
chunks (_NuGets.Dependencies.1, .2, .3, etc.). The parent meta-package
depends on all chunks.

This is self-balancing: as packages grow or shrink, the chunk count
adjusts automatically without any manual curation.

- Delete 8 .Managed/.NativeAssets nuspec files
- Add generic _Dependencies.nuspec template for chunks
- Rewrite nuget-special Cake target with first-fit decreasing bin-packing
- _NativeAssets handling (raw native binaries) is unchanged

feat(content): add CS 320 Software Engineering lesson YAML files

Add three college-level lesson files for the Software Engineering course (CS 320):

- 01_agile_git.yaml: Agile Development & Professional Git Workflow
  6 sections covering Agile Manifesto, Scrum framework (roles/artefacts/
  ceremonies/story-points/burn-down), Git object model & GitFlow/TBD
  branching strategies with Mermaid diagrams, PR anatomy & CODEOWNERS,
  Conventional Commits & SemVer with automated CHANGELOG, and a full
  merge-conflict resolution walkthrough with git bisect.

- 02_design_patterns.yaml: SOLID Principles & Design Patterns
  6 sections covering all five SOLID principles with before/after Python
  examples, Creational patterns (Singleton/Factory/Builder/Prototype),
  Structural patterns (Adapter/Decorator/Facade/Proxy), Behavioural
  patterns (Observer/Strategy/Command/Template Method), code smells with
  refactoring techniques (Extract Method, Replace Conditional with
  Polymorphism, Move Method), and DRY/KISS/YAGNI plus cyclomatic
  complexity measurement with radon.

- 03_testing_tdd.yaml: Test-Driven Development & Software Quality Assurance
  6 sections covering the testing pyramid & AAA vocabulary, full pytest
  fundamentals (fixtures/parametrize/markers/conftest), complete TDD
  Red-Green-Refactor walkthrough building ShoppingCart across 5 iterations,
  unittest.mock (MagicMock/@patch/side_effect/autospec) with HTTP mocking,
  coverage measurement (statement vs branch, pytest-cov, .coveragerc, CI
  enforcement) with flake8/mypy/bandit, and mutation testing with mutmut
  plus Hypothesis property-based testing.

All files validated as structurally correct YAML with 6 sections and 5
quiz questions each, matching the format of existing lesson files.

* docs(squad): formalize PR review gate protocol — CI → review → fix cycle → merge

Per Matthew's directive: all PRs must pass CI, receive unanimous team review
(Aragorn + domain specialists), complete a fix cycle if rejected, then squash
merge with post-merge local main sync. Domain specialists based on PR content:
- DevOps/CI changes → Boromir
- Security changes → Gandalf
- Test changes → Gimli/Pippin
- Backend changes → Sam
- Frontend changes → Legolas

Lockout rule enforced: rejected artifacts cannot be self-revised; fixes route
to a different team member.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(pages): scope deployment to docs/blog/ only

Narrows the upload-pages-artifact path from docs/ to docs/blog/ so
internal files (SECRETS.md, ARCHITECTURE.md, build-log.txt, etc.)
are never published to GitHub Pages.

Also tightens the push trigger path filter to docs/blog/** so the
workflow only fires when blog content actually changes.

commit.message:
Fix proxy-aware equality in views, contains, and list ops

View.__contains__, Item.__contains__, ListProxy.remove/count/index, and
Document.__eq__ all failed or took unnecessary Python round-trips when
the right-hand side was an ItemProxy.

Changes:
- value_eq/table_eq/item_eq now handle ItemProxy via a Rust-only fast
  path (items_structural_eq) before falling back to Python comparison.
- ListProxy.remove split into two phases (find index via shared borrow,
  then remove by index via mutable borrow) to avoid double-borrow panic.
- item_remove replaced by item_remove_at in list_ops.
- Document.__eq__ delegates to table_eq for non-Document comparisons.
- 12 new tests in TestViewContainsProxy covering string, int, bool,
  float, table, array, and cross-document proxy comparisons.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(person-registry): use JdbcTemplate in seeders to bypass Hibernate state detection

Replace JPA repository.save() with JdbcTemplate INSERT ... ON CONFLICT to avoid
Hibernate 6.x PropertyValueException when persisting entities with explicitly-set
UUIDs and @Version/@GeneratedValue annotations.

Also adds JAVA_TOOL_OPTIONS to payment-service to disable grpc-netty native epoll
transport, resolving the SIGSEGV (exit code 139) on container startup.

commit.message:
Refactor combat engagement, add cross-attention module, and fix routing (#183)

* Remove danger tile penalty and move-to-safety reward

The danger tile penalty (-0.5) made melee engagement net-negative:
approaching an enemy cost more than hitting it rewarded (+0.25).
This incentivized camping (standing still, swinging sword repeatedly)
over actively pursuing enemies. See docs/specs/combat-engagement.md.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add entity-spatial cross-attention module

- EntityAttentionEncoder: add forward_with_tokens() returning per-entity
  transformer output + empty mask alongside pooled vector
- EntitySpatialCrossAttention: new 4-head cross-attention where entity
  tokens query spatial feature map (Q from entities, K/V from Conv2d)
  with empty-slot masking and masked mean pooling
- ImpalaCombinedExtractor: integrate cross-attention between entity
  encoder and concatenation, output grows from 335-d to 399-d
- ImpalaResNet: return feature map alongside pooled features for
  cross-attention to use
- ImpalaSharedAgent/ImpalaMultiHeadAgent: forward_with_attention returns
  both spatial and cross-attention weights; get_attention_entropy
  reports cross_attention/entropy and cross_attention/top1_weight
- Debugger: environment_bridge gets get_cross_attention_weights();
  game_view adds entity overlay mode with cycling (Combined → Heads →
  Entities); main_window delegates to cycle_attention_next/prev
- train.py TUI: display cross_attn/entropy and cross_attn/top1 metrics
- triforce.yaml: double main-circuit leg iterations for new parameters
- 13 new tests covering EntityAttentionEncoder tokens, cross-attention
  module shapes/masking/entropy, ImpalaCombinedExtractor output_dim
- cross-attention.md status updated to Implemented

Breaking change: models trained before this commit will not load.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix routing: pre-collect starting room key in Dijkstra

When the agent starts in a room with an uncollected key, the Dijkstra
pathfinder now marks that key as collected in the initial state. This
prevents equal-cost detour routes that leave and re-enter the room
just to pick up the key the agent is already standing on.

Fixes objective suggesting S/E/W from room 0x53 instead of just W.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix empty action mask crash: fallback to all MOVE directions

Link can reach 'impossible' tile positions via sword knockback where
can_link_move() conservatively returns False for all directions. When
combined with no enemies/items (masking SWORD), the entire action mask
becomes empty and crashes training.

Instead of raising RuntimeError, unmask all MOVE directions as a
fallback. The NES will simply not move Link if the direction is truly
blocked, and training continues without interruption.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update model

* Fix unused-argument pylint warnings in action_space.py

Prefix unused parameters in _handle_empty_mask with underscores
to silence W0613 warnings.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Fallback message if no invite_id provided
